steps.title: Identify what the y-intercept represents
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is the point where a graph crosses the $y$-axis. This always occurs when $x = 0$. We need to find the value of $p(0)$ from the table.

steps.title: Read the table at $x = 0$
steps.content: Looking at the table, when $x = 0$, the corresponding value in the $p(x)$ column is $-6$. Therefore, the point is $(0, -6)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
